baseline_note: Every testing benchmark expects Swift Testing (import Testing), not XCTest. All mock repositories are actor-based. ViewModels under test are accessed on @MainActor. Never use @unchecked Sendable.

evals.prompt: Write unit tests for an ExpenseViewModel that loads expenses from a repository and handles a network error
evals.expected_output: Swift Testing suite with @MainActor tests, actor-based mock repository, happy path and failure path
expectations: Uses Swift Testing (import Testing) not XCTest
Mock repository is actor-based (actor MockExpenseRepository)
Includes a test verifying items load correctly on success
Includes a test verifying errorState is set when repository throws
Tests are annotated @MainActor or call ViewModel on main actor
Code is compile-ready Swift

evals.prompt: Create a thread-safe mock for CategoryRepositoryProtocol that I can use across multiple test suites
evals.expected_output: Actor-based mock conforming to CategoryRepositoryProtocol with seed data and configurable error injection
expectations: Mock is actor-based (actor MockCategoryRepository)
Conforms to the same protocol as the real repository
Supports seeding initial data in init
Supports injecting errors for failure-path tests
Does not use @unchecked Sendable
Code is compile-ready Swift

evals.prompt: My ViewModel is getting Swift 6 Sendable and actor isolation errors — fix them without using @unchecked Sendable
evals.expected_output: Corrected code using @MainActor, Sendable protocols, and actors — no suppression hacks
expectations: Annotates the ViewModel class with @MainActor (not just individual methods)
Marks repository protocols as Sendable
Does not use @unchecked Sendable anywhere
Uses actor or value types for any shared mutable state
Code is compile-ready Swift

evals.prompt: Set up constructor injection for ExpenseViewModel so tests can swap in a mock repository
evals.expected_output: ViewModel with protocol-driven init parameter, concrete default for production, injectable for tests
expectations: ViewModel accepts repository via init parameter typed as the protocol
Production default uses the real repository implementation
No global singletons or static state used for DI
Includes a test showing mock injection
Code is compile-ready Swift
